objective.subject: Mathematics
objective.statement: Represent and solve one-step addition, subtraction and missing-number stories within 20 using objects, pictures, equations and spoken reasoning.
misconceptions: Chooses addition or subtraction from one keyword instead of following what happens to the quantities.
Counts all from one after a group is added instead of counting on from the known amount.
In a take-away story, recounts removed objects or loses track of the starting whole.
Treats the equals sign as an instruction to write an answer next rather than a balance showing the same amount.
objective.parent_explanation: Can act out and solve simple adding, taking-away and comparing stories within 20, then match the story to an equation.
objective.teacher_evidence: Stores story structure, concrete model, counting strategy, operation choice and equation separately so reading, speech or fine-motor support does not hide mathematical understanding.

activities.title: I can show what happens in a number story and find the missing amount.
activities.prompt: Show the story in three pictures: what we start with, what changes and what the result is.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Look at the picture, then tap or build how many objects there are.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Activate careful counting, subitising and numeral matching before a story changes the set.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: See the group first. Count each object once if you need to.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Up to ten large objects appear in familiar small-group and ten-frame arrangements.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Show the story in three pictures: what we start with, what changes and what the result is.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Establish a consistent story structure before operation symbols are introduced.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: First show the start. Then show what happens. Last show the amount we need.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Three large panels labelled START, CHANGE and RESULT with replayable narration.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: There are six birds. Three more arrive. How many birds are there now?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Model joining with objects, count-on strategy, spoken equation and equality.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Keep six in your head and count on: seven, eight, nine. Six plus three equals nine.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Six birds remain grouped while three arrive one at a time; the equation appears only after the model.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Build eleven cubes. Move four away. Count what stays and choose the matching equation.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Coordinate starting whole, removed part, remaining part and subtraction notation.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Start with the whole. Move four away once. The cubes still on the mat are left.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A whole tray, away tray and remaining tray preserve every cube.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: One row has eight shells and another has five. How many more shells are in the first row?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Diagnose comparison as a difference situation rather than assuming every subtraction story means objects leave.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Match the rows. The extra unmatched shells show the difference.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Two aligned rows create a visible three-shell comparison gap.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Teach Pip why 7 + 2 = 6 + 3 is true even though there is no answer box at the end.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Secure equality as sameness and prepare flexible missing-number reasoning.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Build both sides. If each side has the same amount, the equation is true.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Two sides of a balance each hold nine counters in different groups.

vocabulary.child_explanation: How many there are when the groups are together.
vocabulary.audio_script: Altogether asks for the whole amount.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Two small baskets joining one large basket.

vocabulary.child_explanation: How many stay after some go away.
vocabulary.audio_script: Left means the amount that remains.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A start tray with some counters moved to an away tray.

vocabulary.child_explanation: How many more one amount has than another.
vocabulary.audio_script: The difference is the gap between two amounts.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Two aligned counter rows with the unmatched extras bracketed.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A number sentence that shows two amounts are equal.
vocabulary.audio_script: An equation uses numbers and signs to show equal amounts.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A balance with one expression on each side.

vocabulary.child_explanation: The number we need to find to make the story and equation true.
vocabulary.audio_script: The missing number completes the story.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: An empty number card linked to a hidden group.

manipulative.purpose: Represent joining, separating and missing-part stories with one conserved set before choosing an equation.
manipulative.interaction_notes: Children tap, drag, keyboard-step or switch-step objects through START, CHANGE and RESULT panels, with optional count-on and count-back audio.
manipulative.accessibility_notes: Large targets, generous spacing, automatic placement, one-object-per-step narration, colour-independent object groups, no time limit and static state changes support motor, visual and processing needs.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use plain story mats, muted object colours, optional sound effects and no confetti, flashing or hurried character reactions.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Replace arriving, leaving and balancing animations with clear START, CHANGE and RESULT still panels.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Speak every story, number and equation with replay; allow the child to answer by moving objects or speaking before reading symbols.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Keep stories to one short sentence, pair text with objects or pictures and label symbols with plus, minus and equals audio.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Show one story stage at a time, hide unused controls and use the predictable build-change-check-say routine.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Allow unlimited thinking time, step-by-step object movement, undo, automatic alignment and repeated narration without penalty.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Preserve correctly placed objects, frame wrong answers as a story-model mismatch and avoid speed, streak or public-comparison pressure.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach join, arrive, leave, left, altogether, more, fewer, same and difference with gestures and matched picture stories.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Represent result-unknown joining stories and move from counting all to counting on.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: start 1-15 + added amount 0-9 + total at most 20 + narrated arrival + build/result/equation
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Use one spoken sentence, preserve the start group visually and include zero naturally without making it a trick.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Represent take-away stories with a fixed starting whole, removed part and amount left.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: start 3-20 + removed amount 0-start + visible away tray + choose/build matching subtraction equation
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Keep removed and remaining objects spatially distinct and narrate actions without overemphasising potentially misleading keywords.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Find a missing start, change or part by rebuilding equal quantities and using known bonds.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: story model + hidden start/add/remove/part + non-terminal blank equation + balance verification
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Include equations such as 5 + ? = 12 and 14 = 9 + ?; read every symbol and accept concrete reconstruction before numeral entry.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Solve how-many-more and how-many-fewer stories by one-to-one matching and identifying the difference.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: two quantities 0-20 + aligned rows + unmatched gap + more/fewer question + subtraction or missing-addend match
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Avoid comparative language that depends on object size or visual density; both rows need equal spacing and audio totals.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Retain story interpretation, equation matching and efficient counting through short mixed review.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: review queue weighted to prior errors + picture-supported join/separate/compare/missing story + model or equation choice
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Do not use keyword-only distractors, provide narration replay and exclude speed scoring or reading-heavy contexts.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Chooses addition or subtraction from one keyword instead of following what happens to the quantities.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The child selects an operation before representing the start and change, or changes the operation when a familiar word is replaced.
repair_sequence: Act or build START, CHANGE and RESULT.
Describe whether the quantity joined, separated or was compared, then select an equation.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The child models and solves a new story with unfamiliar wording but the same structure.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Counts all from one after a group is added instead of counting on from the known amount.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The child recounts the stable start group and loses one-to-one tracking or needs excessive time.
repair_sequence: Circle and name the known start amount.
Touch only each arriving object while saying the next number.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The child counts on accurately in a new joining story while preserving the start group.

misconception_repairs.misconception: In a take-away story, recounts removed objects or loses track of the starting whole.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The answer equals the removed amount or includes objects in both the away and remaining trays.
repair_sequence: Move the removed part once into a clearly labelled away tray.
Count only what remains and recombine to check the whole.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The child solves a new separating story and names whole, removed part and part left.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Treats the equals sign as an instruction to write an answer next rather than a balance showing the same amount.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The child rejects 9 = 6 + 3 or writes a total after both sides of a complete equality.
repair_sequence: Build both sides of the balance.
Count each side and say 'is the same as'.
Complete a missing number in a non-terminal position and verify both sides.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The child accepts and completes a new equation with the expression or missing number on either side.

feedback.companion_prompt: Teach Pip why 7 + 2 = 6 + 3 is true even though there is no answer box at the end.
feedback.parent_summary: Can act out and solve simple adding, taking-away and comparing stories within 20, then match the story to an equation.
feedback.teacher_evidence: Stores story structure, concrete model, counting strategy, operation choice and equation separately so reading, speech or fine-motor support does not hide mathematical understanding.
next_actions: Route keyword guessing to symbol-free START-CHANGE-RESULT modelling.
Route recounting-all to stable-start count-on practice.
Connect secure story structures to independent picture or oral story creation.
Schedule mixed join, separate, compare and missing-number retrieval at 1, 3, 7, 14 and 30 days.

body.prompt: Six birds are on a fence. Three more birds arrive. How many birds are there now?

hints: Keep six in the first group.
Count on three: seven, eight, nine.
questions.explanation: Three birds join the six birds, so there are nine birds altogether.

body.prompt: There are eleven cubes. Four are moved away. Which equation matches the story?

hints: The cubes still on the mat are left.
questions.explanation: Four cubes leave the starting whole of eleven, so seven cubes remain: 11 - 4 = 7.

body.prompt: Five red counters and some blue counters make twelve counters. How many blue counters are there?

hints: Build the whole twelve, then mark the five red counters.
Count the counters in the missing blue part.
questions.explanation: Five and seven make twelve, so the missing number is seven.

body.prompt: Mina has eight shells. Jo has five shells. How many more shells does Mina have?

hints: Match five shells in each row.
questions.explanation: After five shells are matched, Mina has three extra shells, so the difference is three.

reward_payload.explanation: Can act out and solve simple adding, taking-away and comparing stories within 20, then match the story to an equation.
reward_payload.companion_prompt: Teach Pip why 7 + 2 = 6 + 3 is true even though there is no answer box at the end.
